The value is written by the ECU when executing the SendKey SecurityAccess service

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: VIN
Data.Comment: Vehicle Identification Number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the VIN number.

Record data content and format shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer.

It shall not be erased when the ECU is reprogrammed.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUHardwareNumber
Data.Comment: Hardware Number
Data.Description:
This value shall be used by reading services to reference the vehicle-manufacturer-specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be serverspecific and defined by vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: OutputPermanentControlList
Data.Comment: List of Data Identifiers for Input Output Control - The list defined here is AN EXAMPLE.
Data.Description:
Definition of IOControl

Data: IOCTL.DataIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all IO devices that may be
  controlled by InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
 
    0 - DID_Pump
    1 - DID_Wiper

If some of these IO devices have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the IO devices followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: DID_Wiper.Mode
     Type: List
       0 - Slow speed
       1 - High speed

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the InputOutpuControl request, after the IOCTL.OptionRecord.Parameter data.

Data.Name: CT_FrontAlgo_Snapshot1
Data.Comment: Block description of the algorithm. Ask to DIESE department for definition of the content
Data.Description: FRONT ALGORITHM DATA Data recordedWhen is collectedByte numberDetails Algorithm timer BYTE0MSByte- Time of threshold activation since start of front algorithm - Resoution = 1ms/dig BYTE1LSByte Fire Logics BYTE2MSByte- Activated fire logics - Bit0: Fire logic 1 - Bit9: Fire logic 10 - Bit10 - 15: unused - meaning of fire logics is calibration specific BYTE3LSByte X Velocity (1st sensor) BYTE4MSByte- Velocity change in X direction - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ASG4 - in systems w/o ASG4: calculated from ADXL78 - calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE5LSByte X Velocity (2nd sensor) BYTE6MSByte- Velocity change in X direction - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ADXL78 - in systems w/o ASG4: always 0 - calculated on-going with forgetting factor - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE7LSByte Y Velocity BYTE8MSByte- Velocity change in Y direction - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ASG4 - in systems w/o ASG4: always 0 - calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE9LSByte Left B-pillar velocity BYTE10MSByte- Velocity change of left 45Â° B-pillar sensor (zero otherwise) - calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE11LSByte Right B-pillar velocity BYTE12MSByte- Velocity change of right 45Â° B-pillar sensor (zero otherwise) - calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE13LSByte Front Fire Flags BYTE14 - Activated fire flags: - Bit 0: Frontal belt threshold - Bit 1: Frontal driver airbag threshold - Bit 2: Frontal passenger airbag threshold - Bit 3 - 7: unused Front Plausibility BYTE15 - Plausibility flags (calibration specific) - Bit 0: 1st plausibility rule - Bit 1: 2nd plausibility rule - Bit 2 - 7: not used - maximum one bit is set (first fulfilled plausibility rule) Sensor Status BYTE16 - Status of sensors - Bit 0: X sensor defect (ASG4 or ADXL78) - Bit 1: Y sensor defect (ASG4) - Bit 2: X sensor defect (ADXL78) - Bit 3: 1 defect satellite (algo-internally qualified) - Bit 4: 2 defect satellites (algo-internally qualified) - Bit 5: Left B-pillar sensor defect (current sample) - Bit 6: Right B-pillar sensor defect (current sample) - Bit 7: unused Vehicle speed BYTE17 - Vehicle speed at start of frontal algorithm - Resolution: 0.5km/h per digit Index BYTE18Block indexIndex to determine the order of the blocks written in NVM Checksum BYTE19ChecksumChecksum for data valid check

Bit0: Fire logic 1 - Bit9: Fire logic 10 - Bit10 - 15: unused - meaning of fire logics is calibration specific BYTE3LSByte X Velocity (1st sensor) BYTE4MSByte- Velocity change in X direction - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ASG4 - in systems w/o ASG4: calculated from ADXL78 - calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE5LSByte X Velocity (2nd sensor) BYTE6MSByte- Velocity change in X direction - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ADXL78 - in systems w/o ASG4: always 0 - calculated on-going with forgetting factor - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE7LSByte Y Velocity BYTE8MSByte- Velocity change in Y direction - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ASG4 - in systems w/o ASG4: always 0 - calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE9LSByte Left B-pillar velocity BYTE10MSByte- Velocity change of left 45Â° B-pillar sensor (zero otherwise) - calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE11LSByte Right B-pillar velocity BYTE12MSByte- Velocity change of right 45Â° B-pillar sensor (zero otherwise) - calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE13LSByte Front Fire Flags BYTE14 - Activated fire flags: - Bit 0: Frontal belt threshold - Bit 1: Frontal driver airbag threshold - Bit 2: Frontal passenger airbag threshold - Bit 3 - 7: unused Front Plausibility BYTE15 - Plausibility flags (calibration specific) - Bit 0: 1st plausibility rule - Bit 1: 2nd plausibility rule - Bit 2 - 7: not used - maximum one bit is set (first fulfilled plausibility rule) Sensor Status BYTE16 - Status of sensors - Bit 0: X sensor defect (ASG4 or ADXL78) - Bit 1: Y sensor defect (ASG4) - Bit 2: X sensor defect (ADXL78) - Bit 3: 1 defect satellite (algo-internally qualified) - Bit 4: 2 defect satellites (algo-internally qualified) - Bit 5: Left B-pillar sensor defect (current sample) - Bit 6: Right B-pillar sensor defect (current sample) - Bit 7: unused Vehicle speed BYTE17 - Vehicle speed at start of frontal algorithm - Resolution: 0.5km/h per digit Index BYTE18Block indexIndex to determine the order of the blocks written in NVM Checksum BYTE19ChecksumChecksum for data valid check

Data.Name: CT_SideAlgo_Snapshot2
Data.Comment: Block description of the algorithm. Ask to DIESE department for definition of the content
Data.Description: SIDE ALGORITHM DATA Data recordedWhen is collectedByte numberDetails X "Power" BYTE0MSByte- Integrated acceleration values in X direction (from ASG4 or ADXL78) - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE1LSByte left B-pillar gSat BYTE2MSByte- Low pass filtered velocity change of left B-pillar gSat - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE3LSByte right B-pillar gSat BYTE4MSByte- Low pass filtered velocity change of right B-pillar gSat - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE5LSByte left C-pillar gSat BYTE6MSByte- Low pass filtered velocity change of left C-pillar gSat - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE7LSByte right C-pillar gSatT BYTE8MSByte- Low pass filtered velocity change of right C-pillar gSat - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE9LSByte Y Velocity BYTE10MSByte- Low pass filtered velocity change of ECU Y channel (calculated from ASG4) - Resoution: dig (sensor specific) BYTE11LSByte Fire Flags BYTE12 - Activated fire flags: - Bit 0: Left side (ECU-Y based) - Bit 1: Right side (ECU-Y based) - Bit 2: Left side (satellite based) - Bit 3: Right side (satellite based) - Bit 4 - 7: unused Fire Logics BYTE13 - Activated fire logics - Bit0: Fire logic 1 - Bit5: Fire logic 6 - Bit 6 - 7: unused - meaning of fire logics is calibration specific Algo Time pSat BYTE14 - Timer based on pSat satellites - Resolution: 1ms/dig Algo Time gSat BYTE15 - Timer based on gSat satellites - Resolution: 1ms/dig Side Plausibility BYTE16 - Crash side and side plausibility - Bit 0: 0 = left side crash; 1 = right side crash - Bit 1: plausibility threshold left satellite row 1 (left gSat B-pillar) - Bit 2: plausibility threshold right satellite row 1 (right gSat B-pillar) - Bit 3: plausibility threshold left satellite row 2 (left pSat door or left gSat C-pillar) - Bit 4: plausibility threshold right satellite row 2 (right pSat door or right gSat C-pillar) - Bit 5: plausibility threshold Y channel defect (ASG4) - Bit 6: plausibility threshold X channel (ASG4 or ADXL78) - Bit 7: plausibility threshold (calibration specific) Sensor Status BYTE17 - Status of sensors - Bit 0: left satellite row 1 (left gSat B-pillar) - Bit 1: right satellite row 1 (right gSat B-pillar) - Bit 2: left satellite row 2 (left pSat door or left gSat C-pillar) - Bit 3: right satellite row 2 (right pSat door or right gSat C-pillar) - Bit 4: ECU Y channel defect (ASG4) - Bit 5: ECU X channel defect (ASG4 or ADXL78) - Bit 6- 7: unused left pSat value BYTE18 - value of left door pressure sensor - Resolution: digits (sensor specific) right pSat value BYTE19 - value of right door pressure sensor - Resolution: digits (sensor specific) reservered BYTE20 - filler bytes (not used) reservered BYTE21 - filler bytes (not used) Index BYTE22Block indexIndex to determine the order of the blocks written in NVM Checksum BYTE23ChecksumChecksum for data valid check

Data.Name: CT_RearAlgo_Snapshot1
Data.Comment: Block description of the algorithm. Ask to DIESE department for definition of the content
Data.Description:
REAR ALGORITHM DATA
Data recorded When is collected Byte number Details
Algorithm timer   BYTE0 MSByte - Time of threshold activation since start of rear algorithm
- Resoution = 1ms/dig
BYTE1 LSByte
X Velocity (1st sensor)   BYTE2 MSByte - Velocity change in X direction
      - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ASG4
      - in systems w/o ASG4: calculated from ADXL78
- calculated on-going with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE3 LSByte
X Velocity (2nd sensor)   BYTE4 MSByte - Velocity change in X direction
      - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ADXL78
      - in systems w/o ASG4: always 0
- calculated on-going with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE5 LSByte
Left B-pillar velocity   BYTE6 MSByte - Velocity change of left 45Â° B-pillar sensor (zero otherwise)
- calculated from rear algorithm start with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE7 LSByte
Right B-pillar velocity   BYTE8 MSByte - Velocity change of right 45Â° B-pillar sensor (zero otherwise)
- calculated from rear algorithm start with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE9 LSByte
Rear Fire Flags   BYTE10   - Activated fire flags:
     - Bit 0: 1st level rear threshold
     - Bit 1: 2nd level rear threshold
     - Bit 2 - 7: unused
Sensor Status   BYTE11   - Status of sensors
     - Bit 0: X sensor defect (ASG4 or ADXL78)
     - Bit 1: Y sensor defect (ASG4)
     - Bit 2: X sensor defect (ADXL78)
     - Bit 3: 1 defect satellite (algo-internally qualified)
     - Bit 4: 2 defect satellites (algo-internally qualified)
     - Bit 5- 7: unused
Rear Plausibility   BYTE12   - Plausibility flags (calibration specific)
     - Bit 0: 1st plausibility rule
     - Bit 1: 2nd plausibility rule
     - Bit 2 - 7: not used
- maximum one bit is set (first fulfilled plausibility rule)
Vehicle speed   BYTE13   - Vehicle speed at start of frontal algorithm
- Resolution: 0.5km/h per digit
Index   BYTE14 Block index Index to determine the order of the blocks written in NVM
Checksum   BYTE15 Checksum Checksum for data valid check

Data.Name: CT_MIDAlgo_Snapshot1
Data.Comment: Block description of the algorithm. Ask to DIESE department for definition of the content
Data.Description:
SPECIAL PURPOSE ALGORITHM DATA (MID)
Data recorded When is collected Byte number Details
X "Power" (2nd sensor)   BYTE0 MSByte - Integrated acceleration values in X direction
      - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ADXL78
      - in systems w/o ASG4: always 0
- calculated on-going with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE1 LSByte
X Velocity (1st sensor)   BYTE2 MSByte - Velocity change in X direction
      - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ASG4
      - in systems w/o ASG4: calculated from ADXL78
- calculated on-going with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE3 LSByte
Y Velocity   BYTE4 MSByte - Velocity change in Y direction
      - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ASG4
      - in systems w/o ASG4: always 0
- calculated on-going with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE5 LSByte
Algorithm timer   BYTE6 MSByte - Time of threshold activation since start of special purpose algorithm (MID)
- Resoution = 1ms/dig
BYTE7 LSByte
Special Fire Flags   BYTE8   - Activated fire flags:
     - Bit 0: Frontal Near Deploy Threshold
     - Bit 1: Side Crash-Output Threshold
     - Bit 2 - 7: unused
Sensor Status   BYTE9   - Status of sensors
     - Bit 0: X sensor defect (ASG4 or ADXL78)
     - Bit 1: Y sensor defect (ASG4)
     - Bit 2: X sensor defect (ADXL78)
     - Bit 3: 1 defect satellite (algo-internally qualified)
     - Bit 4: 2 defect satellites (algo-internally qualified)
     - Bit 5: Left B-pillar sensor defect (current sample)
     - Bit 6: Right B-pillar sensor defect (current sample)
     - Bit 7: unused
Index   BYTE10 Block index Index to determine the order of the blocks written in NVM
Checksum   BYTE11 Checksum Checksum for data valid check

Data.Name: CT_CrashCurve_Pretrig
Data.Comment: Data of Crash Curve Pretrigger 50ms before Crash detection
Data.Description:
Each value is coded on 2 bytes.

Period of acquisition is 0,5 ms

First sample (byte 1 and 2) is 30 ms before the crash.

Last data (byte 121 ans 122) is at T2 (Time of first event qualification)

Data.Name: CT_Checksum
Data.Comment: Checksum for data valid check

Data.Name: Configuration table.CP Front Left Belt Pyro Rolling Load Limiter System
Configuration table.CP Front Right Belt Pyro Rolling Load Limiter System
CT_CrashCurve_Posttrig
Data.Comment: Data of Crash Curve Post Triger 100ms after Crash detection
Data.Description:
Each value is coded on 2 bytes.

Period of acquisition is 0,5 ms

First sample (byte 125 and 126) is collected at T2 + 0,5ms.

Last data (byte 405 ans 406) is at T2 + 70 ms

T2 is time of first crash event qualification

Data.Name: Configuration table.CP Passenger Seat Position Sensor
Configuration table.CP Center frontal restraints sensor
RoutineIdentifierList.CrashTelegramRequest
Data.Comment: Routine identifier for crash telegram request
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList.CrashTelegramAnswer
Data.Comment: Routine identifier for crash telegram answer
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Item.Text: Satellite Was OFF at Crash Begin

Data.Name: DTCSnapshot.Mileage
Data.Comment: Description of the extendedData Mileage (use by Failure Page to decode data)

Data.Name: IOControlList
Data.Comment: List of Data Identifiers for Input Output Control - The list defined here is AN EXAMPLE.
Data.Description:
Definition of IOControl

Data: IOCTL.DataIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all IO devices that may be
  controlled by InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
 
    0 - DID_Pump
    1 - DID_Wiper

If some of these IO devices have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the IO devices followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: DID_Wiper.Mode
     Type: List
       0 - Slow speed
       1 - High speed

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the InputOutpuControl request, after the IOCTL.OptionRecord.Parameter data.

Data.Name: CT_AlgorythmCalibration_Byte
Data.Comment: ISA Algorithm Calibration Identification Number

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList.CrashCurveRequest_InternalSensor
Data.Comment: Routine identifier for crash curve request
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Comment: Software Number (Supplier)
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: RoutineControlType
Data.Comment: Routine control type - VALUES OTHER THAN 1, 2 & 3 ARE RESERVED AND SHOULD NOT BE USED.

Data.Name: RoutineIdentifierList
Data.Comment: Routine identifier - This is an example. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
Definition of RoutineControl

Data: RoutineIdentifierList

  Contains the list of all routine identifier that may be used with InputOutputControl service.
 
  Example:
  
    0x4501 - ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave

If some of these routine identifer have parameters, you should defined a data with the same name as the routine identifer followed by a dot and a text

  Example:
 
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
  Data: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq

Then, you should add theses data in the "Send tab" of the RoutineControl request, after the RoutineIdentifierList data.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Min
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Max
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Data.Name: ROUTINE_Temperature_Wave.Freq
Data.Comment: This is an example data for RoutineControl request. See RoutineIdentiifierList documentation.

Data.Comment: Routine information
